title: High Volume Data Exfiltration & Information Disclosure Mitigation

steps.message: Block the malicious external IP 45.33.12.98 at the perimeter firewall to halt current exfiltration.
Analyze application logs for SQL injection patterns or verbose error messages (ODBC/Java stack traces) that may have disclosed sensitive data, as described in WAHH Chapter 15.
Retrieve a list of all files and account numbers accessed by the internal source IP prior to exfiltration to determine breach scope.

params.query: search sourcetype=access_logs dest_ip=45.33.12.98 | table uri, client_ip, bytes

steps.message: Implement code changes to suppress verbose error messages and disable debug functionality in the production environment to prevent further information disclosure (WAHH p.627).

params.description: Disable customErrors and stack trace output

steps.message: Review identified vulnerabilities (Broken Access Control/SQLi) and approve the remediation plan to harden application entry points.
Initiate data breach notification protocols for 5GB exfiltration incident per regulatory requirements.
Final sign-off on incident closure and verification that information disclosure vectors are strictly controlled.
